metadata.summary: Dual-optimization for search engines and AI assistants: technical SEO, on-page, hreflang, schema, llms.txt, Core Web Vitals, accessibility.

content.title: SEO & AEO Mastery
content.summary: Comprehensive SEO and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) methodology covering technical SEO, on-page optimization, multilingual hreflang, schema markup, llms.txt, Core Web Vitals, accessibility, and social meta tags. Optimizes content for both traditional search engines (Google, Bing) and AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude).

why.p: Modern traffic comes from two distinct discovery paths: traditional crawl-index-rank and AI-assisted answer generation. Sites optimized for only one path miss the other entirely. The dual-optimization approach ensures visibility in both — the same structural clarity, authoritative content, and proper markup that satisfies Google's E-E-A-T also enables AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended) to cite and reference your content.

list.item: Running a full SEO audit on a new or existing site
Implementing technical SEO foundations (robots.txt, sitemap, HTTPS, Core Web Vitals)
Setting up multilingual sites with hreflang
Adding schema markup (Article, Organization, FAQ, BreadcrumbList, HowTo)
Creating llms.txt for AI crawler guidance
Optimizing for AI Overviews, featured snippets, and zero-click answers
After a Core Web Vitals regression (LCP/INP/CLS thresholds breached in CrUX)
After a Google algorithm update or sudden traffic drop (≥20 percent week-over-week)
Content strategy decisions (use content-marketer methodology instead)
Paid search campaigns (use ppc-manager)
Link building (use growth-seo-link-building)
When the site has no indexable pages yet — fix technical blockers first
When the site is intentionally non-indexable (intranet, staging, private app)

rule.statement: Optimize content for both traditional search engines (Google, Bing) AND AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Both paths require clear structure, authoritative content, proper markup, and fast loading. The same technical quality that satisfies Google also enables AI crawlers to cite the content.

list.item: Technical: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, crawl budget, Core Web Vitals, HTTPS, canonicals
On-Page: semantic HTML, schema markup, headings, meta tags, internal links
Multilingual: hreflang, URL structure, x-default, language-region pairs
AI/AEO: llms.txt, structured data, entity optimization, citable answers
Social: Open Graph, Twitter Cards, LinkedIn metadata
Accessibility: WCAG 2.2, ARIA, screen readers, semantic markup
Performance: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1 (Core Web Vitals 2026)

rule.statement: Signal Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness in content and site structure.
rule.rationale: Google uses E-E-A-T as a quality evaluator. Missing any dimension — particularly Trustworthiness (HTTPS, contact info, privacy policy) — reduces ranking potential regardless of content quality.

list.item: Experience: first-hand knowledge, case studies, original data, screenshots
Expertise: author credentials, depth of content, citations, sameAs links
Authoritativeness: backlinks, brand mentions, industry recognition
Trustworthiness: HTTPS, contact info, privacy policy, no deceptive patterns

how-to.p: Run the audit-fix-verify loop end-to-end. Each step has a concrete check and a verification command.
how-to.step: Crawl the site and capture a baseline. Run Screaming Frog or sitemap-driven curl loop, export status codes, titles, meta descriptions, H1, canonicals, hreflang.
Validate robots.txt and sitemap.xml. Confirm AI bots are allowed (or explicitly disallowed by policy) and sitemap is referenced from robots.txt.
Audit Core Web Vitals from CrUX field data and lab data. Use PageSpeed Insights API for top 10 templates (home, category, article, product, etc.).
Validate structured data with Google Rich Results Test and Schema.org validator. Cover Article/BlogPosting, Organization, BreadcrumbList, FAQ, HowTo where applicable.
Verify hreflang on every multilingual page. Check bidirectional pairing and x-default fallback.
Publish or refresh llms.txt at the site root with curated entry points for AI crawlers.
Audit on-page SEO: one H1, descriptive title (≤60 chars), meta description (≤160 chars), canonical present, OG/Twitter image present.
Run accessibility scan (axe-core, Lighthouse a11y) and fix WCAG 2.2 AA blockers.
Submit updated sitemap in Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools, request reindexing for changed templates.
Re-crawl and diff. Confirm zero regressions, zero new 4xx/5xx, no missing canonicals.

checklist.check: robots.txt present at /robots.txt, references sitemap, no accidental Disallow: /
sitemap.xml lists only canonical, indexable URLs, returns 200, <50k URLs per file
Every page has a unique <title> (≤60 chars) and meta description (≤160 chars)
Exactly one <h1> per page, descriptive and keyword-aligned
Canonical <link rel="canonical"> present and self-referential or correctly redirected
HTTPS enforced site-wide; HSTS header set; no mixed content warnings
hreflang tags valid, bidirectional, with x-default for international sites
Schema.org JSON-LD validates in Rich Results Test (no errors, warnings reviewed)
Core Web Vitals pass: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1 on 75th percentile mobile
Open Graph and Twitter Card meta complete with og:image (≥1200x630)
llms.txt present at site root, lists primary entry points and license terms
WCAG 2.2 AA compliance: contrast, focus, alt text, landmark roles
XML sitemap submitted to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools
404 page returns 404 (not 200), 301 redirects used for permanent moves
No noindex on production canonical URLs (regression test)

example.title: robots.txt with AI crawler policy
example.code:
User-agent: * Allow: / Disallow: /admin/ Disallow: /cart/ Disallow: /*?sort= Disallow: /*?filter=
# AI crawlers — explicit allow for citable indexing User-agent: GPTBot Allow: /
User-agent: ClaudeBot Allow: /
User-agent: PerplexityBot Allow: /
User-agent: Google-Extended Allow: /
User-agent: CCBot Allow: /
Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap-news.xml
example.note: Replace Allow with Disallow for any bot you want to exclude from training/answer use. Keep classic search bots (Googlebot, Bingbot) allowed unconditionally.

example.title: llms.txt at site root
example.code:
# Example Site
> One-line description of what this site offers and who it serves.
## Primary
- [Home](https://example.com/): main landing page - [About](https://example.com/about): mission, team, contact - [Docs](https://example.com/docs/): canonical documentation index
## Articles
- [Topic Hub A](https://example.com/topic-a/): pillar page for topic A - [Topic Hub B](https://example.com/topic-b/): pillar page for topic B
## Optional
- [Changelog](https://example.com/changelog/) - [Pricing](https://example.com/pricing/)
## License
Content is © Example, Inc. Citation with link is permitted. No verbatim republication beyond fair-use snippets.
example.note: Spec: https://llmstxt.org. Keep entries curated — this is the AI equivalent of a sitemap, not a full URL list.

example.title: Article + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD
example.code:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@graph": [
    {
      "@type": "Article",
      "@id": "https://example.com/post-slug/#article",
      "headline": "Post Title (≤110 chars)",
      "description": "One-sentence summary, ≤160 chars.",
      "datePublished": "2026-05-01T09:00:00+00:00",
      "dateModified": "2026-05-01T09:00:00+00:00",
      "author": {
        "@type": "Person",
        "name": "Author Name",
        "url": "https://example.com/authors/author-slug",
        "sameAs": ["https://www.linkedin.com/in/...", "https://github.com/..."]
      },
      "publisher": {
        "@type": "Organization",
        "name": "Example",
        "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://example.com/logo.png"}
      },
      "mainEntityOfPage": "https://example.com/post-slug/",
      "image": ["https://example.com/post-slug/hero.jpg"],
      "inLanguage": "en"
    },
    {
      "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
      "itemListElement": [
        {"@type": "ListItem", "position": 1, "name": "Home", "item": "https://example.com/"},
        {"@type": "ListItem", "position": 2, "name": "Blog", "item": "https://example.com/blog/"},
        {"@type": "ListItem", "position": 3, "name": "Post Title", "item": "https://example.com/post-slug/"}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</script>
example.note: Validate with Rich Results Test before deploy. Add FAQ/HowTo entities only when the page actually contains those sections.

example.title: hreflang in <head> for a multilingual page
example.code: <link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://example.com/en/page/" /> <link rel="alternate" hreflang="uk" href="https://example.com/uk/page/" /> <link rel="alternate" hreflang="pt-PT" href="https://example.com/pt/page/" /> <link rel="alternate" hreflang="x-default" href="https://example.com/en/page/" /> <link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/en/page/" />
example.note: Pairs MUST be bidirectional: every alternate must list every other alternate including itself. x-default is required for international sites.

example.title: OG + Twitter Card minimum set
example.code: <meta property="og:type" content="article" /> <meta property="og:title" content="Post Title (≤60 chars)" /> <meta property="og:description" content="≤160 chars summary." /> <meta property="og:url" content="https://example.com/post-slug/" /> <meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/post-slug/og.jpg" /> <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" /> <meta property="og:image:height" content="630" /> <meta property="og:locale" content="en_US" /> <meta property="og:site_name" content="Example" /> <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" /> <meta name="twitter:site" content="@example" /> <meta name="twitter:creator" content="@author" />

section.name: aeo-answer-block
section.p: To win AI Overviews, featured snippets, and zero-click answers, place a self-contained answer block near the top of the page (40-60 words), followed by depth.

example.title: Citable answer pattern
example.code:
What is X?

X is … (one-sentence definition, 20-30 words). It works by … (one sentence, 20-30 words). The key benefit is …

How does X work?

Step 1 …

Step 2 …

FAQ

Is X free?

Direct answer in one short paragraph.
example.note: Use plain question-form H2/H3 for FAQ entries and pair with FAQPage schema. AI assistants prefer self-contained answers that do not require scrolling context.

section.name: prompts
section.prompt:
You are an SEO auditor. Given the JSON below from Screaming Frog + PageSpeed Insights + Rich Results Test, output a prioritized fix list in this exact format:
CRITICAL (fix this week): - <issue>: <impact> — file/url: <path> — fix: <one line>
HIGH (fix this month): - ...
MEDIUM: - ...
For each item include: rule violated (E-E-A-T / CWV / hreflang / schema / a11y), expected verification command, and a one-line success criterion.
Input: <<<DATA>>>

Rewrite the page below so it wins AI Overviews. Constraints: - First paragraph is a self-contained 40-60 word answer to the page's primary query. Begin with the entity defined in bold. - Each H2 is a question form ("How …", "Why …", "What …"). - Add a FAQ block (3-5 Q/A) at the bottom. - Output the rewritten Markdown plus a JSON-LD block with Article + FAQPage. - Preserve all factual claims; do not invent statistics or quotes.
Page: <<<MARKDOWN>>>

list.item: Google Search Console — indexing, Core Web Vitals, coverage errors, manual actions
Bing Webmaster Tools — Bing/ChatGPT-search indexing visibility
PageSpeed Insights / CrUX API — performance and CWV field data
Rich Results Test — schema markup validation
Schema.org Validator — generic structured data validation
Ahrefs / Semrush — backlinks, keywords, competitive audits
Screaming Frog — technical crawling and link audit
Lighthouse / Lighthouse CI — performance, a11y, SEO, best-practices in CI
WAVE / axe-core — accessibility testing
linkinator — broken link detection
IndexNow — instant indexing for Bing, Yandex, Seznam
Keyword research and data extraction: haiku — list compilation, lookup tasks
Analyzing SEO metrics and patterns: sonnet — comparative analysis, audit synthesis
Developing SEO strategy and market positioning: opus — complex strategy formulation

verify.step: Run sitemap fetch: curl -s https://SITE/sitemap.xml | xmllint --noout - (exit 0 = well-formed XML).
Run robots fetch and confirm Sitemap: directive is present and AI bot policy matches intent.
Run PageSpeed Insights for the 5 top templates (home, category, article, product, contact). Confirm performance, accessibility, SEO, best-practices each ≥ 90 on mobile.
Run Rich Results Test on 3 representative URLs (home, article, product). Confirm zero errors and warnings reviewed.
Run Lighthouse CI in a build job; fail the build on any score below the configured budget.
Spot-check 5 multilingual URLs: every alternate referenced points back, x-default present, canonical self-referential or to chosen language.
Confirm Search Console shows the sitemap submitted, no Index Coverage errors trending up, CWV "Good" share > 75 percent on mobile.
Confirm Bing Webmaster Tools sitemap submitted and IndexNow key file (if used) reachable at /<key>.txt.
Diff the new crawl vs baseline: zero new 4xx/5xx, zero canonical changes outside the planned set, zero new noindex on indexable URLs.
Cite-check: search Perplexity and ChatGPT for 3 brand queries; confirm site is cited or appears as a source. If not, audit content depth and entity markup.

list.item: Lighthouse mobile scores ≥ 90 across performance, accessibility, SEO, best-practices for top 5 templates
CrUX 75th percentile passes all three Core Web Vitals on the origin
Rich Results Test reports zero errors on home, article, and product templates
Search Console Index Coverage shows zero "submitted, not indexed" trending up week-over-week
llms.txt and robots.txt both reachable, parseable, and reference the sitemap
WCAG 2.2 AA scan returns zero blocker-level violations
Brand queries surface the site as a source in Perplexity/ChatGPT search where citations are shown

antipattern.description: Disallowing Googlebot or all AI crawlers in robots.txt by accident (e.g. shipped staging robots to prod)
antipattern.reason: Site disappears from index within days; recovery requires reindex requests and may take weeks

antipattern.description: Relying on client-side rendering without prerendering for crawl-critical pages
antipattern.reason: Some crawlers (notably AI bots and Bingbot) execute JavaScript inconsistently; content may be missed

antipattern.description: Stuffing schema with FAQ/HowTo blocks that do not match visible page content
antipattern.reason: Google penalizes mismatched structured data; rich results are revoked and trust score drops

antipattern.description: Multiple H1 tags or missing H1, duplicate titles across paginated archives
antipattern.reason: Confuses both classic and AI crawlers about page topic, dilutes ranking signals

antipattern.description: hreflang pairs that are not bidirectional or omit x-default
antipattern.reason: Google ignores the cluster and serves wrong-language pages to international users

antipattern.description: Optimizing only for Google and ignoring AI crawlers (no llms.txt, no entity markup, content gated behind login walls)
antipattern.reason: Lose the growing share of zero-click and AI-assisted traffic; competitors get cited instead
